answers.where: Mine Cards mining screen with a top miner action stage and lower idle board.
answers.action: Watch Surface Stone mining and work toward Copper Pickaxe; this is more readable than v004 but still not clear enough for a first-time player.
answers.response: Progress and geode reward appear in the stage, with improved actor/target placement.
answers.reward: Stone, coins, XP, and upgrade cost are visible, but the reward loop still relies on text and placeholder panels.
answers.game_look: It is closer to a game screen than v004, but still reads as a procedural prototype rather than a polished voxel idle RPG.

issues.text: Stage rescue improved actor scale and removed the disconnected status badge, but the top stage is still mostly empty procedural chrome rather than a strong game scene.
The miner/rock relationship is clearer than v004, but the hit moment still lacks a proper staged impact pose/effect and portrait crops the character into the header area.
The lower board still has same-weight rectangular active activity, future chips, node cards, upgrade, and nav; hierarchy is serviceable but not product quality.
Runtime still depends on procedural placeholder UI and reused placeholder markers; a generated/artist UI and icon family is required before feature expansion.
Portrait is captured and usable, but not phone-first or Capybara-like enough; the character/action zone does not yet dominate first read.

not_covered.reason: No dedicated normal tick reward sequence reviewed in this pass.
Visible but still weak due placeholder badge/chip hierarchy.
Covered by prior affordance pass, not this stage rescue.

problem: Stage layout tuning improved the actor/target relationship, but the screen still fails product read because it lacks real stage art/UI/icon family and portrait-first composition.
next: Stop numeric layout tuning. Create the generated/artist UI and stage/icon source family, then integrate it into the native screen and rerun landscape/portrait product gate.
